name: @radzor/workflow-engine
description: Define and execute multi-step workflows with branching, parallel execution, and error handling. Supports conditional logic, retries, and workflow persistence for complex orchestration scenarios.

inputs.name: maxConcurrency
inputs.description: Maximum number of parallel steps that can run simultaneously.

inputs.name: defaultTimeout
inputs.description: Default timeout in milliseconds for each step.

inputs.name: retryAttempts
inputs.description: Default number of retry attempts for failed steps.

outputs.name: workflowResult
outputs.description: Final result of a completed workflow execution.

fields.name: steps
fields.description: Ordered list of step results with status and output.

fields.name: output
fields.description: Aggregated output from all completed steps.

events.name: onStepComplete
events.description: Fired when an individual step finishes successfully.

events.name: onWorkflowComplete
events.description: Fired when the entire workflow finishes.

events.name: onWorkflowFailed
events.description: Fired when a workflow fails due to a step error.

actions.name: defineWorkflow

params.name: definition
params.description: Workflow definition with steps, branches, and configuration.

actions.description: Register a workflow definition and return its ID.

actions.name: execute

params.name: workflowId
params.description: ID of the registered workflow to execute.

params.name: input
params.description: Input data passed to the first step.

actions.description: Execute a registered workflow with optional input data.

params.name: executionId
params.description: ID of the running execution to pause.

params.name: executionId
params.description: ID of the paused execution to resume.

actions.name: cancel

params.name: executionId
params.description: ID of the execution to cancel.

actions.description: Cancel a running or paused workflow execution.

actions.name: getStatus

params.name: executionId
params.description: ID of the execution to inspect.

actions.description: Get the current status of a workflow execution.

llm.constraints: Workflows are stored in-memory; state is lost on process restart. Steps run sequentially by default; use parallel groups for concurrency. Each step handler receives the aggregated output of prior steps.
